name: Rabalsorri Trail
name_original: Sendero de Rabalsorri – circular desde Sahún (Puro Pirineo 06)
description: This difficult 15.5-mile mountain biking loop from Sahún offers stunning Benasque Valley views and a challenging descent.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Sahún are situated in the heart of the Benasque Valley within the Aragonese Pyrenees, offering a diverse landscape for riders. The region is characterized by high-mountain scenery, including towering peaks, the Ésera river, and varied terrain ranging from forests to open landscapes. Routes often traverse a mix of roads, 4WD tracks, and technical single-track pathways, providing a range of challenges. Best mountain bike trails around Sahún The most popular mountain bike trail is Rabalsorri Trail, a 15.5 miles (25.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 32 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers extensive views of the Benasque Valley. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Castejón de Sos – Linsoles Reservoir loop from Eriste, a difficult 33.5 miles (53.9 km) path. This route loops around the Linsoles Reservoir, providing varied terrain and scenery. Local mountain bikers also love the Benasque Village Center – Puente de Cubera loop from Eriste, a 27.4 miles (44.0 km) trail leading through the Benasque Valley, often completed in about 3 hours 38 minutes. Mountain biking around Sahún is defined by high-mountain landscapes, the Ésera river, and challenging rocky passages. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easier paths to technically demanding routes. The routes in Sahún are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 1700 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Sahún's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there around Sahún?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;14 mountain bike trails&lt;/strong&gt; around Sahún, offering a diverse range of experiences for riders in the Benasque Valley.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of mountain bike trails in Sahún?
A: The trails around Sahún cater to various skill levels. You'll find &lt;strong&gt;2 easy routes&lt;/strong&gt;, &lt;strong&gt;1 moderate route&lt;/strong&gt;, and &lt;strong&gt;11 difficult routes&lt;/strong&gt;. Many of the difficult trails require good fitness and advanced riding skills due to technical sections of dirt, stone, and rocky passages.

Q: Are there any loop mountain bike trails in Sahún?
A: Yes, many routes around Sahún are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17548250" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castejón de Sos – Linsoles Reservoir loop from Eriste&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 53.9 km difficult route. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1398307" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benasque Village Center – Puente de Cubera loop from Eriste&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 44 km through the Benasque Valley.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the mountain bike trails around Sahún?
A: Mountain bikers can expect breathtaking views of the Benasque Valley and the high mountain scenery of the Pyrenees. Trails lead through diverse environments, from forests to open landscapes, and past traditional villages. The area is characterized by towering peaks, the Ésera river, and parts of the Posets-Maladeta Natural Park.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Sahún?
A: While many trails around Sahún are rated as difficult, there are &lt;strong&gt;2 easy routes&lt;/strong&gt; available that might be suitable for families or less experienced riders. These trails offer a gentler introduction to mountain biking in the region.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or landmarks I might see while mountain biking near Sahún?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You might encounter the stunning Barbarisa waterfalls, or pass by high-mountain lakes (ibones) like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2730495" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibonet de Batisielles&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/795895" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibón of Plan (Basa de la Mora)&lt;/a&gt;. The trails also offer views of the massive Sierra of Chia and the Ésera River.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Sahún?
A: The mountain bike trails around Sahún are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of &lt;strong&gt;4.7 stars from over 80 reviews&lt;/strong&gt;. Riders often praise the challenging terrain, the stunning high-mountain landscapes, and the diverse mix of roads, 4WD tracks, and single-track pathways.

Q: Are there any particularly long or challenging routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19887116" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Linsoles Reservoir – Linsoles Reservoir loop from Eriste&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 52.5 km route with significant elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40910168" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benasque Village Center – Puerto Ampriu loop from Eriste&lt;/a&gt;, covering 37.5 km with nearly 1000m of ascent.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Sahún?
A: The Benasque Valley, where Sahún is located, is a high-mountain area. While specific recommendations depend on snowmelt and weather, generally late spring through early autumn offers the most favorable conditions. Winter mountain biking may be possible on lower elevation trails, but higher routes can be affected by snow and ice.

Q: Are there any cultural attractions near the mountain bike trails in Sahún?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, Sahún maintains its traditional charm. You can explore the 12th-century Romanesque parish church of San Juan Bautista, the remains of the Romanesque-Lombard hermitage of San Aventín, or visit the Sanctuary of Nuestra Señora de Guayente and the chapel of San Pedro Mártir. The nearby town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3480038" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castejón de Sos&lt;/a&gt; also offers cultural insights.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails around Sahún offer varied terrain, often traversing a mix of roads, 4WD tracks, and single-track pathways. You can expect technical sections of dirt, stone, and rocky passages, particularly on the more difficult routes, providing a true test of mountain biking skills.
tips: Comfortable path parallel to the river Ésera at the height of Sesué. It is one of the few opportunities to go near the river after the Eriste reservoir since the river is boxed in and the paths are usually well above it.

It's an uphill track and even we did it on an e-bike it's doable on a normal bike, the views of the valley are impressive but there are some steep parts. There are some trails going down but they are not so cool, really the cool thing are the views not the trails.

Every night of San Juan the traditional "falles" (fire festivals) are celebrated. Although this custom is carried out in numerous towns in the Pyrenees, the ritual in Sahún is somewhat different since it includes the unique flipping of the fallas.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Sendero de Rabalsorri trail?
faqs.answer: The circular route starts and ends in Sahún. You can typically find parking within the village, which serves as the main access point for this trail and other routes in the Puro Pirineo area.

faqs.question: Is the Sendero de Rabalsorri trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is rated as having "Alta" (High) physical difficulty and "Difícil" (Difficult) technical difficulty. It demands very good physical condition and significant bike experience. There are sections with tricky rocks and steep descents where you might need to dismount and push your bike.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: Given its location in the high-mountain environment of the Benasque Valley, the best time to ride this trail is generally during the late spring, summer, and early autumn months. This avoids snow and ice, ensuring safer and more enjoyable conditions.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: No, typically you do not need a specific permit or to pay an entrance fee to ride the Sendero de Rabalsorri trail. It is part of the public trail network in the Puro Pirineo MTB center.

faqs.question: Is the Sendero de Rabalsorri trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed on trails in the Pyrenees, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially given the challenging terrain and potential for encountering wildlife or other riders. Ensure your dog is fit enough for a difficult mountain bike ride.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a varied mix of terrain: approximately 17% asphalt, 52% track (pista), and 31% singletrack (sendero). You'll encounter dirt, stone, and technical passages, including sections with "tricky rocks" that require careful navigation. A particularly steep and technical section is near the Barbarisa waterfalls during the descent.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights to look out for on this route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights along the route include riding along the Ésera River Trail, enjoying the Best views of the Benasque Valley, and passing by the historic Church of San Juan de Sahún.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Sendero de Rabalsorri loop?
faqs.answer: Komoot estimates the duration at approximately 3 hours and 32 minutes. However, due to its difficulty and technical sections, it's generally advised to allow 3-4 hours to comfortably complete the 25.2 km route, depending on your skill level and stops.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: You can expect picturesque views as the trail descends along the Ésera River, passes through characteristic meadows and birch forests, and runs parallel to the Surri ravine. The Benasque Valley offers spectacular alpine scenery with numerous peaks over 3,000 meters, along with traditional villages and natural landscapes.

faqs.question: Does the Sendero de Rabalsorri connect with other mountain bike trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route shares an initial section with the legendary Rabaltueras route. It also covers sections of other official trails, including La Ribagorza. BTT. Ruta 21. Vuelta a la Siera de Chia, La Ribagorza. BTT. Ruta 22. Eriste - Sesué - San Sadurní, and La Ribagorza. BTT. Ruta 31. Castejón de Sos – Villanova – Sesué – Castejón de Sos.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities or services available in Sahún?
faqs.answer: Sahún is a traditional village in the Benasque Valley and serves as a base for many outdoor activities. You can typically find basic amenities such as cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options to refuel and relax after your ride.

waypoints.name: Best views of the Benasque Valley
Church of San Juan de Sahún
